Interview Title: PLEASE RESPOND – Interview Invitation for African Australian Inclusion Program
Interview Subject: PLEASE RESPOND – Interview Invitation for African Australian Inclusion Program
Interview Email: Dear Agnes Congratulations on reaching the next stage of the recruitment process for the position of Advisor, The Village - (AAIP2019MELB209) in the African Australian Inclusion Program. Please find below the important information regarding your Interview: Date – Friday 4th October 2019 Time – 9.45am to 10.30am Location – Room 03.112 at 700 Bourke St​. Melbourne. (Please note, if you are applying from interstate, it is not a requirement for you to attend the interview in person. Please confirm if you would like to interview via Skype when you RSVP for your interview). CONTACT ON DAY: Please contact one of our team members on info@corporatediversity.org.au or 03 9421 7600 Within 24 hours, please confirm receipt of this email and confirmation of your attendance at the interview by sending an email to info@corporatediversity.org.au If you do not RSVP, we will assume you do not wish to continue with your application. During this recruitment process we need to be able to contact you so please keep your phone on and monitor your emails closely. FINAL: Please check travel times when planning for your interview so as to allow enough time if there is heavy traffic or delays with public transport. We recommend you arrive 15 minutes before the above scheduled interview time. ONLINE TESTING As part of the recruitment process, all candidates are required to complete online testing specific to the role they have applied for. What do I need to do before I get started? • Use a PC or Mac with Mozilla Firefox or Internet Explorer installed. • Disable any pop-up blockers- for example, Google or Yahoo Toolbar. • To give yourself the best opportunity to do well on the assessment, we recommend that you complete the assessment in a quiet area where you will be free of interruptions. 1. Online Abilities Assessment - this test will give us and the NAB Hiring Manager an insight in to your problem solving and analytical skills, which are critical skills for this role. You will have 48 hours to complete this assessment, and it will take approximately 45 minutes . To register yourself for testing: 1. Click on link https://nab.maptq.com/nab/home/en/register 2. Enter ALL personal details 3. Enter Access Code: AAIP@Careers 4. Check the box to accept the terms of agreement 5. Click SAVE Once you have registered as per the above instructions, you will receive an email from Corporate Diversity Partnerships to complete your online test. Please check your junk mail/spam folder if you do not receive this email. If you experience any technical difficulties including starting, accessing or completing the assessments please email nab@cut-e.com outlining the nature of your query. 2. Excel test - Shortly you will receive a separate email from Testgrid with instructions on how to complete the Excel online testing. Please complete the online testing within 48 hours of receiving the email. Should you have issues accessing the test, please contact the Testgrid team directly via support@testgrid.com . Please note you only need to complete the Online Abilities Assessment test once. If you have already completed this test as part of recruitment for another AAIP role, you can disregard. Any other online AAIP test (e.g. Excel) you receive will be specific to the role you have applied for and you are required to complete. If you are unable to gain access to the internet during the time periods required for your tests, please let Jesuit Social Services know as soon as possible at info@corporatediversity.org.au . PREPARING FOR YOUR INTERVIEW: You are invited to attend a NAB B.E.I Skills Workshop. Often past behaviour is an excellent predictor of future performance so we use an interview technique called Behavioural Event Interviewing (BEI). To help you understand more about the process, NAB is running a BEI Skills Workshop. We encourage you to attend. The workshop is not compulsory but will be helpful to you. Please find attached some information to assist you in preparing for your interview. Date: Thursday 19 September 2019 Time: 9:00am to 12:00pm Where: The Bowl, The Academy - NAB, 500 Bourke Street, Melbourne R.S.V.P: Book your place at the Workshop by clicking here Thank you and should you have any questions, please feel free to email Corporate Diversity Partnerships at Jesuit Social Services - info@corporatediversity.org.au

Good Afternoon Michelle, As discussed, I am pleased to invite you to attend an interview for the role of Customer Service Officer on 4 th September at 10:30am Further details are below: Address: Singleton Visitor Information and Enterprise Centre – 23 Maitland Road, Singleton. Ask for me at reception. Interviewer/s: Brendon Burness - Team Leader Customer Service, Shaina Way – Senior Customer Service Officer, Carissa Starr – Project & Systems Officer Singleton Council uses the STAR interview technique. The STAR (Situation, Task, Action, Result) technique is used to gather all the relevant information about a specific capability that the job requires. Each question will rely on you providing an example as part of your answer. Some tips on this style of interviewing are below; Situation: The interviewer wants you to present a recent challenge and situation in which you found yourself Task: What did you have to achieve? The interviewer will be looking to see what you were trying to achieve from the situation Action: What did you do? The interviewer will be looking for information on what you did, why you did it and what the alternatives were Results: What was the outcome of your actions? What did you achieve through your actions and did you meet your objectives? What did you learn from this experience and have you used this learning since? Please do not hesitate to contact me should you have any questions.
